name: Cappuccino Biscotti
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Espresso, strong brewed and chilled
1 tablespoon Espresso, strong brewed and chilled
recipeInstructions:
To toast & skin hazelnuts: Place them in a single layer on a baking sheet. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and bake for 10-15 minutes or until lightly colored and skins blistered. Wrap the nuts in a kitchen towel, let them steam for 1 minute, then rub the nuts to remove as much skin as possible. Cool.
1. In a bowl fitted with a paddle attachment, blend together flour, sugar, baking soda, baking powder, salt, cinnamon, and ground cloves until well combined.
2. Whisk espresso, milk, egg yolk, and vanilla in a separate bowl. Add the mixture to the flour mixture, beating until a dough forms. Stir in hazelnuts and chocolate chips.
3. Turn dough onto a floured surface and knead several times. Divide into two portions.
4. Shape each portion into a log, 12 inches long by 2 inches wide. Space logs at least 3 inches apart on a large buttered and floured baking sheet.
5. Bake in the center of a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 35 minutes. Let cool on the sheet with a rack underneath for about 10 minutes.
6. Lower the oven temperature to 300°F (150°C). Cut logs crosswise into 3/4 inch slices and arrange cut sides down on the baking sheet. Bake for 5-6 minutes on each side or until pale golden.
7. Transfer biscotti to racks to cool completely before storing in airtight containers. Makes about 32 biscuits.
